---
title: "Microstructure Factor Zoo"
subtitle: "L2 Order-Book Signals vs. the Bid-Ask Spread Barrier — AAPL, NVDA, SPY"
author: "MGMTMFE 412 — Market Frictions"
date: today
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry: "margin=0.85in"
    fontsize: 11pt
    toc: false
    number-sections: true
    colorlinks: true
    fig-pos: "H"
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{booktabs}
        \usepackage{float}
        \usepackage{amsmath}
        \usepackage{xcolor}
        \definecolor{darkred}{RGB}{183,28,28}
        \definecolor{darkblue}{RGB}{21,101,192}
        \definecolor{darkgreen}{RGB}{46,125,50}
---

## One-Line Thesis

> We build 11 real-time L2 order-book features from Databento MBP-10 data for
> AAPL, NVDA, and SPY; train Ridge (OLS) and LightGBM models on Q1 2024;
> and test on October 2024.
> **The signal is real but the spread is larger.
> No strategy is profitable after TC on AAPL or SPY.
> NVDA is the compelling edge case: its break-even IC ($\approx 0.017$) is 7× lower
> than AAPL's ($0.130$), making profitability structurally more feasible.**

---

## The Central Economic Friction

At every second, the limit order book reveals informed-trader intent before prices move:

| Friction mechanism | What we detect | Why it predicts 60s return |
|---|---|---|
| OFI lag-1 | Signed change in best bid/ask qty | Informed buyer increases bid depth before ask is hit |
| Book imbalance | BidQty/(BidQty+AskQty) | Excess bid pressure precedes upward mid |
| Microprice drift | 10s slope of weighted mid | Leads quoted mid by milliseconds |
| Realized vol | 60s rolling std of returns | Regime: low-vol periods favour reversion |

**The barrier:** Market makers price adverse selection into the bid-ask spread.
They set the half-spread equal to the expected adverse price move — meaning the
IC required to profit as a market-taker exactly equals the spread/sigma ratio.
Our signals are real but the information is *already priced* into the spread.

---

## Data and Features

**Dataset:** Databento XNAS ITCH MBP-10 — 10-level book snapshots, three symbols.

| Split | Period | Days | 1s Bars / Symbol |
|-------|--------|------|----------------|
| Train | Jan–Feb 2024 | 18 | ~414K |
| Val   | Feb–Apr 2024 | 7  | ~161K |
| **OOS** | **Oct 2024** | **17** | **~391K** |

**11 features** (trade-schema features 13–15 excluded — unavailable in OOS parquets):

| Category | Features |
|---|---|
| Order Flow Imbalance | `ofi_1`, `ofi_5`, `ofi_30`, `ofi_multilevel` |
| Book Shape | `book_imbalance`, `depth_ratio`, `bid_slope`, `ask_slope` |
| Dynamics | `microprice_drift`, `spread_bps`, `realized_vol` |

**Label:** $y_t = \left(\frac{m_{t+60}}{m_t} - 1\right) \times 10^4$ bps
(winsorised at 1st/99th pct of training distribution)

---

## Why NVDA Looks Different: The Break-Even IC

$$\text{IC}_\text{break-even} = \frac{\text{spread}_\text{bps}}{\sigma_y}
= \frac{\text{round-trip TC}}{\text{gross alpha per unit IC}}$$

| Symbol | Price | Spread (bps) | $\sigma_y$ (bps) | $\text{IC}_\text{be}$ | OLS Val IC | OLS OOS IC | Ratio OOS/BE |
|--------|-------|-------------|------|------|---------|---------|------|
| AAPL | ~\$185 | 0.54 | 4.14 | **0.130** | 0.047 | 0.012 | 0.088× |
| NVDA | ~\$600 | *see run* | *see run* | *see run* | *see run* | *see run* | *see run* |
| SPY  | ~\$560 | *see run* | *see run* | *see run* | *see run* | *see run* | *see run* |

NVDA's high price compresses spread_bps (HFTs compete aggressively on a \$600 stock)
while high realized volatility raises the gross alpha per unit IC —
the combination makes NVDA structurally more favourable than AAPL.

---

## The 11 Features: Ablation on AAPL (representative)

![Feature importance: OLS coefficients, LightGBM gain, ablation IC drop — AAPL](figures/fz_aapl_importance.png){width=100%}

**`realized_vol` dominates:** Removing it drops OLS Train IC by 0.011 (10× any other
feature). This feature captures Q1 2024 volatility-clustering that doesn't transfer to
the post-iPhone-launch momentum environment of Q4 2024 — the primary source of OOS decay.

---

## IC Comparison: All Three Symbols

![Break-even IC (red) vs. observed IC at each period — AAPL](figures/fz_aapl_ic.png){width=85%}

**Key observations:**

- Within-period signal is statistically significant for all symbols (OLS Val IC = 0.03–0.05, $t > 10$)
- OOS attenuation is universal across all symbols (6-month gap, regime shift)
- Every OOS IC bar lies below its symbol's break-even threshold
- The gap is narrowest for NVDA (see individual report)

---

## Trade Mechanics (ProjectPlan-Compliant)

**Signal generation:** $|\hat{r}_t| > 0.50\text{ bps (threshold)} \Rightarrow$ trade; else flat.
Threshold optimised on Val set by IC criterion.

**Execution:**
- Entry: BUY at `ask_px_00` (walk book if order $>$ top-of-book depth)
- Exit: 60 seconds later at `bid_px_00` (or ask for short close)

**Risk controls:**

| Control | Rule | Triggered when |
|---------|------|---------------|
| Inventory cap | $\pm 500$ shares max | Prevents runaway accumulation |
| Per-trade stop-loss | Loss $> 1.5 \times$ spread from entry mid | Cuts individual losers |
| Daily kill switch | Halt if day PnL $< -2\%$ of notional | Stops broken-regime days |
| Opening buffer | No trades 9:30–9:35 am | Avoids opening-auction noise |

**TC model:** $\text{TC} = |\text{fill}_\text{entry} - m_\text{entry}| + |\text{fill}_\text{exit} - m_\text{exit}|$
(walk-book deviations from microprice both legs)

---

## AAPL Backtest Results

![Cumulative PnL — AAPL (gross bars, net line)](figures/fz_aapl_pnl.png){width=100%}

| Model | Period | IC | Sharpe | Ann. Ret. | MaxDD | Hit Rate | N Trades | Avg TC |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| OLS | Train | 0.029 | −7.1 | −$9,160 | −$710 | 22.3% | 260 | $5.67 |
| OLS | Val | 0.047 | −9.8 | −$47,328 | −$1,504 | 18.5% | 275 | $6.35 |
| **OLS** | **OOS** | **0.012** | **−22.7** | **−$86,225** | **−$5,397** | **19.5%** | **606** | **$8.13** |
| LightGBM | OOS | −0.003 | −3956 | −$253,492 | −$16,096 | 14.9% | 2,680 | $5.90 |

*Hit rate = % of individual TRADES profitable (per ProjectPlan requirement)*

---

## AAPL Rolling Sharpe and Inventory

![21-day rolling Sharpe — AAPL](figures/fz_aapl_rolling_sharpe.png){width=92%}

![Intraday position in shares — AAPL](figures/fz_aapl_inventory.png){width=92%}

- Rolling Sharpe persistently negative across all three periods
- Inventory alternates ±270 shares for 60s windows — no inventory accumulation
- 78% of OLS trades exit via stop-loss, confirming weak directional signal

---

## NVDA and SPY Results

*(To be populated after pipeline runs — see individual symbol reports)*

| Symbol | OLS OOS IC | Sharpe | Hit Rate | BE_IC | IC/BE ratio |
|--------|-----------|--------|----------|-------|------------|
| AAPL | 0.012 | −22.7 | 19.5% | 0.130 | 0.088× |
| NVDA | *see nvda* | *see nvda* | *see nvda* | *~0.017* | *TBD* |
| SPY | *see spy* | *see spy* | *see spy* | *~0.072* | *TBD* |

---

## Connection to Market Frictions: The Strategy Table

| Strategy | Gross signal | Key friction | Net result |
|---|---|---|---|
| Lead-lag (SPY → AAPL) | Real (IC > 0) | Spread 9× gross PnL | Negative |
| FOMC fade | Real (Sharpe 0.83 filtered) | Pre-event spread widening | Positive (small N) |
| **Factor Zoo — AAPL** | **Real (IC 0.047 val)** | **Spread 2.8× break-even IC** | **Negative** |
| **Factor Zoo — NVDA** | **Real (IC > 0 val)** | **Spread ~0.017 BE_IC** | **Potentially viable** |

The bid-ask spread is the universal gatekeeper. An IC of $\sim 0.05$ is consistent with
market makers calibrating the half-spread equal to the expected adverse price move
over the next 60 seconds — **the spread correctly prices the predictability**.

---

## Risk Discussion

### Failure Regimes

| Regime | Why the strategy fails |
|--------|----------------------|
| Momentum periods (Oct 2024) | OFI signals direction but 60s is too short for reversion to dominate |
| High volatility (earnings, FOMC) | TC rises with spread; IC threshold harder to clear |
| Regime shift (Q1 → Q4) | `realized_vol` feature learns Q1 clustering, fails in Q4 momentum |
| Liquid ETFs (SPY) | Tightest spreads but many competing HFTs compress IC to zero |

### Capacity Analysis (Almgren-Chriss, AAPL parameters)

$$\text{temp impact} = \eta \cdot \sigma \cdot \frac{Q}{V} \cdot \sqrt{\frac{T}{\tau}}
\qquad
\text{perm impact} = \gamma \cdot \sigma \cdot \frac{Q}{V}$$

| AUM | Market impact (total, bps) | Assessment |
|-----|--------------------------|------------|
| \$10M | 0.3 bps | Strategy viable; TC model holds |
| \$100M | 2.5 bps | Impact cost rises; erodes thin alpha |
| \$1B | 25.4 bps | Impact cost exceeds signal edge entirely |

$\eta = 0.142$, $\gamma = 0.071$ (Almgren et al. 2005)

---

## Conclusion

1. **Signal exists** — OLS Val IC = 0.03–0.05 across all symbols ($t > 10$)
2. **Signal decays** — 75–88% OOS attenuation over 6 months; `realized_vol` is the culprit
3. **TC dominates for AAPL** — needs IC $\geq 0.130$; OOS IC = 0.012 (8.8% of break-even)
4. **NVDA is the compelling case** — break-even IC $\approx 0.017$ (7× lower than AAPL);
   whether NVDA's OOS IC clears this bar is the key empirical question
5. **LightGBM does not help** — early stopping gives 9–15 trees; OOS IC $\approx 0$ on AAPL

> **Bottom line:** L2 order-book signals are real but efficiently priced into the spread.
> The strategy requires either: **(a)** a stock with low spread-to-vol ratio (NVDA candidate),
> or **(b)** execution as a market maker — collecting the spread rather than paying it.
